free_text: Systematics of Pion and Proton Interactions with Ni
Nuclides
#doi:10.1103/PhysRevLett.35.641
High-energy (P3) proton channel and low-energy
pion channel at LAMPF.
Single gamma-ray spectra gated only on
macroscopic beam pulses.
The gamma-rays were detected at 90 degrees by
a shielded 50-cm3 Ge(Li) detector.
gamma-ray lines were identified and assigned to
particular final nuclides on the basis of their
energies. Production cross sections for various
residual nuclides were computed assuming isotropic
angular distribution.
BP
OS. Error column added, other corrections.
Enriched to more than 99.8%,
thickness was 8 g/cm2
Table 1, page 642.
Total cross section of 310 mb is given. However,
total defined as not including inelastic cross
sections.
The secondary gamma-rays produced by neutrons
may be considerable for (n,n') and (n,alpha)
reactions. A rough estimate indicates 50-100% of
inelastic gamma-rays and 10-20 mb of the 55Fe yield
could be of secondary origin.
Most of the cross sections are accurate at
+-20% except 56Fe and 48Ti. No uncertainty details are
given.
Second error column added, SAMPLE coding
updated
Enriched to more than 99.8%,
thickness was 8 g/cm2
Table 1, page 642.
Total cross section of 277 mb is given. However,
total defined as not including inelastic cross
sections.
The secondary gamma-rays produced by neutrons
may be considerable for (n,n') and (n,alpha)
reactions. A rough estimate indicates 50-100% of
inelastic gamma-rays and 10-20 mb of the 55Fe yield
could be of secondary origin.
Most of the cross sections are accurate at
+-20% except 59Co, 54Fe, 48Ti and 47Ti. No uncertainty
details are given.
Second error column added, SAMPLE coding
updated
Data taken from tab. 1 of the reference
Positive and negative pions were produced
using low energy pion channel at LAMPF. Pions are
produced by 800 MeV protons from linac in rotating
wheel target and focused in an achromatic optical
system consisting of four bending magnets and
entrance and exit quadrupole doublets.
Enriched to more than 99.8,
thickness was 8 g/cm2
The secondary gamma-rays produced by neutrons
may be considerable for (n,n') and (n,alpha)
reactions. A rough estimate indicates 50-100% of
inelastic gamma-rays and 10-20 mb of the 55Fe yield
could be of secondary origin.
Second error column added, FLAG added,
missing data line (Fe54) added, BIB updates
